meta.title: R129 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Mercedes R129 engine — M103, M104, M112, M119, M113, M120. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and the M119 chain guide risk explained. Get free quotes from 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes R129 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned R129 engine typically costs £2,000–£14,000 supply only [MB‑QUOTE], depending on engine — the M104 straight‑six runs £2,000–£4,500, while the M120 V12 in the SL 600 can cost £6,000–£12,000. Fitting adds £780–£1,430. Use our calculator for a personalised quote.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes R129 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M104 3.2‑litre straight‑six (SL 320, 1993–1998) is the most dependable choice — it's robust, smooth, and lacks the V8's timing‑chain guide worries or the V12's coil‑pack costs. The later M112 V6 (1998–2001) is also reliable, with cheap parts and simple maintenance.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes R129?

acceptedAnswer.text: M119 V8s suffer timing‑chain guide wear; M120 V12s have expensive coil pack failures; M104 straight‑sixes can develop wiring harness degradation; and M112/M113 (facelift) units can suffer water pump failure. Regular servicing is critical across all engines.

It depends on the engine. The M104 straight‑six is generally excellent. The M119 V8 is robust but chain guides are a known weakness. The M120 V12 is an engineering masterpiece but demands expensive upkeep. The M112 V6 and M113 V8 are the most modern and reliable. Overall, a well‑maintained R129 can be exceptionally durable.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes R129 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine is an outright "avoid" — but a high‑mileage SL 600 (M120 V12) without documented coil pack replacement should be approached with caution. The M119 V8 is a great engine, but budget for chain guides. The M104 is the safe bet for budget‑conscious buyers.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my R129 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Key warning signs include: rattling on start‑up (M119 chain guides), misfires or rough running (M120 coil packs), electrical gremlins or rough idle (M104 wiring harness), or coolant loss with no visible leak (M112/M113 water pump). If you experience any of these, get a diagnosis immediately — early intervention saves thousands.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes R129 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value and condition. Against a well‑kept £12,000 SL 500, a £6,500 M119 recon is 54% of the car's value — safe if the car is otherwise exceptional. Against a tired £5,000 example, the same recon is 130% — walk away. Use our 70% rule as your guide. For the M120 V12, many enthusiasts will pay for the rebuild due to the engine's rarity.

mainEntity.name: How long do Mercedes R129 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With proper maintenance, the M104 straight‑six can exceed 250,000 miles, and the M112 V6 is similarly durable. The M119 V8 is capable of 200,000+ miles with regular chain guide maintenance. The M120 V12 is robust but expensive to maintain; coil pack replacement is a recurring cost.

mainEntity.name: What are the engine eras of the R129?

acceptedAnswer.text: The R129 has three distinct engine eras: (1) 1989–1993 — M103/M104 straight‑sixes and M119 V8; (2) 1993–1998 — M104 2.8L/3.2L, M119 V8 continued, M120 V12 added; (3) 1998–2001 — M112 V6 replaces M104, M113 V8 replaces M119, M120 V12 continues. This page covers all three.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes R129 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate (visible through the windscreen) and in the vehicle documentation. The code is stamped on the engine block — on M103/M104 it's on the left side, on M119/M120 it's on the right side, and on M112/M113 it's on the front left. Tell us your variant and registration year, and we'll identify it for you.

hero.h1: Mercedes SL R129 Engines — The Complete UK Guide

engineDatabase.h2: Mercedes SL R129 Engine Codes — The Complete Database

confidenceScore.title: Confidence Score
confidenceScore.text: All ratings: Expert‑knowledge tier, derived from cross‑referencing specialist and owner sources — confirm against the Model Page before publishing. The M120 V12 and M119 V8 receive lower ratings due to documented coil pack / timing‑chain guide wear.

overview.intro: The R129 is the fourth generation of the Mercedes‑Benz SL‑Class, a two‑seat roadster produced from May 1988 to 2001. Designed by Bruno Sacco and Johann Tomforde, the R129 replaced the long‑running R107 and introduced several innovations including a fully automated electro‑hydraulic fabric roof and pop‑up roll hoops. The engine range evolved across three distinct eras — from the original M103/M104 straight‑sixes and M119 V8, through the M104 updates and M120 V12 addition, to the final‑facelift M112 V6 and M113 V8 engines. The R129 was replaced by the R230 in 2002.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Mercedes‑Benz R129 • Predecessor: R107 • Successor: R230 • Sibling body styles in this generation: none (single roadster body style).
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: No R129‑specific enquiry figure is available; the R129 remains a sought‑after classic, with the V8 and V12 variants generating the most enthusiast interest.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Early 24V — more power, watch the loom
Modern Six — reliable V6, cheap parts
V8 Icon — smooth, watch the guides
Modern V8 — reliable SOHC, fewer worries
V12 Legend — engineering icon, high costs
AMG Rare — high performance, very high cost

economicsRule.title: The R129 rule
economicsRule.text: A £3,500–£6,500 M119 recon against a £5,000–£15,000 R129 sits at 23–130% of the car's value. On a sub‑£6,000 example, that brushes our 70% Mercedes threshold — we'll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well‑kept £12,000+ example, the same recon drops to a routine 29–54% — a repair decision, not a financial one. For the M120 V12, the calculus is different — many enthusiasts will pay for the rebuild due to the engine's rarity and desirability.

keyTakeaways.question: Which R129 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: M104 2.8 straight‑six or M112 V6 — simple, robust, cheapest to maintain.

keyTakeaways.question: Which R129 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: M120 V12 — coil packs, mounts, and specialist labour add up fast.

keyTakeaways.question: Which R129 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: SL 500 (M119 V8) — V8 prestige without V12 running costs, if the chain guides are done.

problems.title: M119 Timing‑Chain Guide Wear
problems.rootCause: The timing‑chain guides on the M119 V8 can become brittle with age and heat cycling. If they fail, the chain can slip, leading to catastrophic valve‑to‑piston contact.
problems.recommendation: If your R129 V8 has over 80,000 miles and no documented timing‑chain guide replacement, budget for the work proactively — an £800–£1,800 repair is far cheaper than the £3,500+ replacement that follows a failure.

cta.label: Compare M119 engine prices for your R129 →

problems.title: M120 V12 Coil Pack Failure
problems.rootCause: The M120 V12 has 12 individual coil packs that can fail with age. Parts are expensive and often specialist‑only.
problems.recommendation: If you're buying an R129 V12, budget for a full ignition system inspection — coil pack replacement is inevitable on higher‑mileage examples. This is the price of V12 ownership.

cta.label: Compare M120 engine prices for your R129 →

problems.title: M104 Wiring Harness Degradation
problems.rootCause: The biodegradable wiring harness insulation used on M104 engines can degrade with age, leading to electrical gremlins, rough running, and potential engine damage.
problems.recommendation: If you own an M104‑powered R129, have the wiring harness inspected — an £800–£1,500 replacement is cheaper than the £2,000+ damage that follows an electrical failure.

cta.label: Compare M104 engine prices for your R129 →

problems.title: M113 / M112 Water Pump Failure
problems.rootCause: The plastic water pump impeller on M112 and M113 engines can degrade, leading to coolant loss and potential overheating.
problems.recommendation: Have the cooling system inspected at every service — a £300–£600 water pump replacement is cheap insurance against a £1,500+ head‑gasket repair.

cta.label: Compare M112 / M113 engine prices for your R129 →
